items.title: Oil Holds Decline as Investors Weigh Iran War Impact on Supply
items.summary: Oil was little changed after a volatile session in which investors weighed signs of less-tight conditions against rising military threats to energy infrastructure across the Middle East.

items.title: Asian Private Bankers Go on Blitz to Calm Private Credit Nerves
items.summary: Private bankers across Asia are scrambling to contain client anxiety as redemption pressures ripple through the $1.8 trillion private credit market, even in a region seen as more insulated from the recent turmoil.

items.title: Bankman-Fried’s Mom Told to Not Call Court on Son’s Behalf
items.summary: FTX co-founder Sam Bankman-Fried’s mother may have been a law professor at Stanford University. But the judge who sentenced her son to 25 years in prison said she can’t act on his behalf in his bid for a new trial.

items.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang sees at least $1 trillion of AI chip revenue opportunity through 2027
items.summary: Nvidia's chief Jensen Huang forecasts a massive one trillion dollar revenue opportunity for its AI chips by 2027. This projection highlights Nvidia's strong position in the AI chip market. The company is also expected to reveal details about its next-generation AI chip, Feynman. Nvidia's strategy of reinvesting profits into the AI ecosystem appears to be paying off, despite increasing competition.

items.title: US stocks rebound on AI optimism revival; Dow rises 387 pts, Nasdaq, S&P 1%
items.summary: Wall Street surged Monday, led by AI-related stocks like Meta and Nvidia, as Meta announced significant layoffs and Nvidia unveiled new components. Oil prices dipped amid easing Middle East conflict concerns, offering market relief. The Dow, S&P 500, and Nasdaq all closed higher, reflecting positive investor sentiment.

items.title: Investors take to digital gold in a big way
items.summary: Digital gold purchases in India saw a significant surge in 2025. Younger investors are increasingly opting for small, online gold purchases over traditional jewellery. This trend is driven by convenience and accessibility. Experts believe government regulations will further boost this digital gold market.

items.title: Fading flames, rising heat: LPG crisis leaves restaurants and staff on edge
items.summary: Restaurant owners are struggling to get LPG supplies. This shortage is causing operational issues and impacting business. Many fear job cuts and salary reductions if the situation continues. Some businesses are exploring alternative cooking methods. The industry anticipates a difficult period ahead due to these ongoing supply challenges.

items.title: Revamped Stand-Up India scheme soon: Nirmala Sitharaman
items.summary: Replying to a supplementary question in the Lok Sabha, Sitharaman said the modified programme would incorporate lessons from the earlier scheme, which was discontinued in April 2025 after nine years. "With studies done by Niti Aayog and others, this scheme is being redrafted and soon the Cabinet will be given a proposal with which we will launch a new scheme to continue with Stand-Up India, which benefitted SC/ST and women all over the country," she said.

items.title: Air India, AI Express to operate 44 West Asia flights
items.summary: On Tuesday, travelers will find 44 flights from Air India and Air India Express heading to West Asia. However, the escalating regional conflict has led Dubai airport to impose restrictions on foreign carrier flights. In response, IndiGo and SpiceJet have temporarily suspended their services to Dubai.

items.title: Amazon knocks on DoT's door to get Satcom nod fast
items.summary: Amazon is pushing India for faster approval of its satellite communication permit. The company has sent multiple letters to the government. This comes as Amazon lags behind its satellite launch schedule. It faces strong competition from Elon Musk's Starlink. Amazon aims to offer significantly more capacity than Starlink in India.

items.title: SC grills Sebi over lingering probe against Sandesara brothers despite Rs 5,100 cr settlement
items.summary: In a surprising turn of events, the Supreme Court has scrutinised Sebi's ongoing actions against the directors of Sterling Biotech. This review arises following the directors’ hefty ₹5,100 crore settlement payment. Earlier, the court had invalidated all related cases. Sebi's investigation concerns the suspected routing of funds.

items.title: Exporters, freight forwarders seek government help as war surcharges on cargo surge
items.summary: Amid escalating geopolitical tensions, Indian exporters and freight forwarders are appealing for government support. Shipping lines worldwide are imposing significant war-risk surcharges on Middle Eastern shipments, often doubling the previously established rates.

items.title: Magicbricks and realtors' body tie up for data-driven info exchange
items.summary: Magicbricks and NAREDCO have joined forces to forge a strategic alliance designed to invigorate India's real estate sector. This one-year agreement positions Magicbricks as a key knowledge and communication partner, tasked with launching research reports and regular newsletters.

items.title: HC upholds SECI's reallocation of solar capacity to Adani Co
items.summary: The Delhi High Court has backed Solar Energy Corporation of India's 2023 move. This decision reallocated 2.33 gigawatts of solar power capacity from Azure Power India to Adani Green Energy. A public interest litigation challenging this reallocation was dismissed. The court found no evidence of loss to the public exchequer. The reallocation involved a reduction in the power purchase price.

items.title: Bank-NBFC co-lending pacts may be expanded to aid MSMEs
items.summary: A parliamentary panel suggests boosting bank and NBFC co-lending to help MSMEs access cheaper credit. It also proposes listing profitable regional rural banks and reinforcing state-run general insurers. The committee highlighted a significant credit gap for small businesses and a decline in insurance penetration. Recommendations include developing climate risk insurance and using business correspondents to reach rural areas.

items.title: At bottom of the pyramid, 21 million small borrowers have dropped out
items.summary: Twenty-one million small borrowers exited formal finance. Lenders stopped new loans to those with overdue payments after a microfinance crisis. Many have returned to private moneylenders. This trend reflects tighter lending rules and market contraction. Loan disbursements saw a recent increase, indicating a slow recovery.

items.title: Easier FDI norms notified for border country investors
items.summary: India has updated its foreign direct investment policy. Investments from countries sharing a land border now face stricter government scrutiny. Overseas companies with up to 10% Chinese shareholding can invest via the automatic route, subject to limits. However, entities registered in China or other bordering nations require government approval. This change aims to regulate investments from neighboring countries.

items.title: RBI seeks banks’ views on AI-based facial recognition tools at ATMs, branches to curb fraud
items.summary: As per the draft guidelines, a bona fide victim, being an individual person and having lodged a complaint involving gross loss of an amount up to Rs 50,000 on account of fraudulent electronic banking transaction (s), would be compensated 85% of the net loss amount (calculated after reducing recoveries made, whether before or after paying the compensation, from the gross loss amount), or Rs 25,000, whichever is less, once during his or her lifetime.

items.title: Audit gaps at Big 4 affiliates
items.summary: India's financial watchdog, NFRA, has released inspection reports on major audit firms. These reports highlight issues with audit independence, internal governance, and scrutiny of related-party transactions. The regulator aims to improve audit quality and protect investors. While some improvements were noted, further strengthening of controls is required across these firms.

items.title: PHDCCI urges RBI to boost MSME credit, fix LoC rules
items.summary: The PHD Chamber of Commerce and Industry has approached the Reserve Bank of India. They are seeking increased credit for small businesses. The chamber also wants the RBI to resolve issues with letters of credit. Inconsistent handling of document discrepancies by banks is a concern. Unfair practices by rating agencies are also highlighted. Clearer guidelines for banks are requested.

items.title: 'No decisions yet on capping refinery prices': Sujata Sharma
items.summary: Fuel retailers are facing losses as pump prices remain low despite rising refining costs. Reports suggested state oil companies might pay less to other refiners. However, the government has not approved any decision on capping refinery prices. India has sufficient crude oil and refiners are working at high capacity.

items.title: ReNew sells 11% stake in unit for $95 million
items.summary: ReNew has announced the sale of an 11% equity stake in its commercial and industrial power generation division, attracting $95 million from three strategic investors: Leapfrog Investments, Emerging Market Climate Action Fund, and Carlyle AlpInvest. This transaction values the division at an impressive $850 million and underscores the vital role of the C&I sector in India's journey towards decarbonization.

items.title: West Asia war: Specialty fertiliser imports 10-20% costlier, cos to step up production
items.summary: Imported fertilizer prices have surged 10-20 percent following the Iran war. This is impacting supplies of key farm nutrients. Indian companies are now increasing production of specialty fertilizers and enhancing stocks. Farmers are exploring cost-effective domestic options. Dealers are also stocking these products as a hedge against potential shortages.

items.title: Despite PNG meter connection, nearly 40% customers can't access piped gas
items.summary: Many homes with piped natural gas meters are not receiving gas. The regulator is urging city gas companies to fix this issue. This comes as the government encourages a shift from LPG cylinders to piped gas. Companies are offering incentives to encourage more users. The focus is now on activating existing connections rather than just adding new ones.

items.title: As West Asia fire rages, green transition chatter gets louder
items.summary: As tensions rise in West Asia, the urgency to transition from fossil fuels is becoming increasingly clear. A pivotal conference hosted by Colombia and the Netherlands is on the horizon, while Brazil is laying down a blueprint for an equitable energy transition. Meanwhile, India's focus could shift towards cutting down on oil and gas imports.

items.title: Small investors fear US SEC will drive corporate gadflies to extinction
items.summary: Small investors' ability to influence corporate boards through shareholder proposals is facing significant restrictions. Proposed new rules and higher ownership thresholds could sideline individual shareholders, activists, and faith-based groups. This move, supported by business groups, aims to curb what they deem excessive or irrelevant proposals, sparking concerns about silencing diverse voices.

items.title: 4,816 Inspections Since March 12: Yogi-led UP govt takes strict action against black-marketers

items.title: India’s horticulture production to increase marginally in 2025-26: First Advance Estimate
items.summary: India's horticulture production is set to remain steady in 2025-26. Vegetable output is expected to rise, driven by onions and potatoes. Fruit production will see a slight increase. Flower production may see a minor dip. Spice output is projected to grow significantly, with gains in cumin, ginger, garlic, and turmeric. This sector is crucial for boosting farmer incomes.

items.title: London Metal Exchange electronic trading resumes after nearly 3-hour outage

This disruption halted price updates for key metal contracts, prompting the LME to declare a Disruption Event. Electronic trading resumed at 1730 GMT on a secondary engine.
